hero.badge: Engineer @ NVIDIA — open to side projects
hero.title: Projects & Demos
hero.subtitle: A collection of visual experiments spanning algorithms, pathfinding, fractals, and computer vision — built for the joy of making things work.

categories.label: Chess
categories.title: Chess Engines & Visualizations
categories.description: Exploring classic board problems and building a full chess game from scratch, including advanced rules and position analysis.

projects.title: Knight's Tour — 50×50
projects.description: Visualization of the Knight's Tour problem solved on a massive 50×50 chess board, watching the knight traverse every square exactly once.

projects.title: Full Chess Game
projects.description: A complete chess implementation featuring En Passant, Castling, and a full King Check detection mechanism.

projects.title: PGN Parsing + Square Control
projects.description: A Magnus Carlsen game visualized through custom PGN parsing, with a square-control heatmap overlay.Red — abandoned squares with no attackers or defenders.Green — squares equally attacked and defended.

categories.label: Pathfinding
categories.title: Path Finding Algorithms
categories.description: Classic pathfinding algorithms visualized across randomly generated mazes and weighted grids.

projects.title: Dijkstra on a Random Maze
projects.description: Dijkstra's algorithm discovering the shortest path through a procedurally generated maze, one cell at a time.

projects.title: Pathfinding Showcase
projects.description: A broader tour of pathfinding approaches with obstacles and weighted terrain.White — impassable barriers.Brown — weighted paths, like a traffic jam.

categories.label: Fractals
categories.title: The Mandelbrot Set
categories.description: A deep dive into one of mathematics' most iconic fractals — rendered, colored, and explored.

projects.title: Mandelbrot Set Explorer
projects.description: Rendering and zooming into the infinite detail of the Mandelbrot set — where simple iteration produces stunning complexity.

categories.label: Computer Vision
categories.title: Computer Vision + Arduino
categories.description: A simple but genuinely fun experiment bridging software vision with physical hardware.

projects.title: Arduino Computer Vision
projects.description: Live computer vision driving an Arduino — simple in concept, but surprisingly satisfying to watch in action.
